design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed=hash('Super vs Siri|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Siri|2026-07-06T07:28:30.797152+00:00') -> architecture=workflow-command-center; AIDA Check: Attention(hero), Interest(proof & sources), Desire(media & motion), Action(CTA); Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk; Hero Math Verification: clamp headline to 2–3 lines at max-width 1120px; Bento Density Verification: grid-auto-flow:dense present; GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics; Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, white secondary CTA verified.
headline: Super vs Siri — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Siri. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Siri

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison for people deciding between Super and Siri. Siri excels as a voice‑first assistant embedded across Apple devices. Super is purpose‑built for repeated computer‑use workflows, with agents that operate real interfaces and reuse a computer-use cache so work compounds instead of repeating.
